title: RapidMeta Renal | Sparsentan (Dual ETA/AT1 Antagonist) in IgA Nephropathy v1.0

pico.pop: Adults with biopsy-confirmed primary IgA nephropathy with persistent proteinuria (UPCR >=1.0 g/g) on stable maximally-tolerated RAAS blockade
pico.int: Sparsentan 400 mg orally once daily (dual endothelin-A and angiotensin-II type 1 receptor antagonist)
pico.comp: Irbesartan 300 mg orally once daily (active comparator)
pico.out: Percent change in urine protein:creatinine ratio (UPCR) at week 36 (interim primary); confirmed eGFR slope at week 110 (final primary)
pico.subgroup: Baseline UPCR (>=1.75 vs <1.75 g/g), baseline eGFR (>=60 vs <60), prior immunosuppression

real_data_entries.name: PROTECT
real_data_entries.group: Adults with primary IgA nephropathy on RAS blockade, sparsentan 400 mg vs irbesartan 300 mg (week 36 UPCR ratio interim primary; week 110 eGFR slope final)

allOutcomes.title: UPCR ratio at week 36 (sparsentan / irbesartan, geometric mean ratio; interim primary)
Confirmed eGFR slope at week 110 (sparsentan minus irbesartan; mL/min/1.73m^2/year, MD; final primary)

real_data_entries.snippet: Source: Heerspink HJL et al. Lancet 2023;401:1584-1594 (PROTECT, week 36 interim primary). Final 110-week eGFR slope reported in Heerspink 2024.

extra_replaces: The Voclosporin Evidence in Lupus Nephritis
The Sparsentan Evidence in IgA Nephropathy
